Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Mark Basil Pappas, Esq. 707-745-8206 mbpappaslaw@comcast.net for Plaintiff.
Kathleen Darmagnac, Esq. 925-974-8600 kdarmagnac@leonealberts.com of Leone & ALberts for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions.

Taken Under Submission.** (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

**After the hearing, an Order GRANTING SFUSD'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FOR SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $550.00 is signed/filed. (see scanned order for details)**

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-04-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Mark Basil Pappas, Esq. 707-745-8206 mbpappaslaw@comcast.net for Plaintiff. (Counsel had technical difficulties and was heard after the Court adopted the tentative ruling)
Kathleen Darmagnac, Esq. 510-332-3208 kdarmagnac@leonealberts.com for Defendant.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. The case is first called at 9:30 am. There is no Appearance by plaintiff's Counsel. At the second calling of the case at 9:46 am, there is still no appearance by Plaintiff's Counsel Atty. Papas. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions.

The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Defendants' motion to compel further responses to form and special interrogatories and requests for production of documents is granted. Plaintiff shall pay $550 in sanctions.***At 9:55 am, after Ms. Darmagnac has been excused, Mr. Pappas overcomes technical difficulties and is heard. The Court informs him of what transpired and allows Counsel to stipulate with Opposing Counsel re: a new hearing date due to technical problems this morning.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)***

No order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-22-2020 Continued To Aug-04-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Atty. KATHLEEN DARMAGNAC, Esq. of Leone & Alberts (Tel: 925-974-8600) for Defendant
San Francisco Unified School District;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions.

The Court notes it received an email yesterday from plaintiff's counsel indicating that plaintiff contests the tentative ruling and does not stipulate to the matter being decided by the judge pro tem. The Court notes that no appearance has been made this morning by or for plaintiff.

The matter is continued as a non-stipulated matter to August 4, 2020 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion.

Judge Pro Tem David McDonald to prepare a report & recommendation.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

------

Matter re-called at 1:00 p.m.

APPEARANCES VIA TELEPHONE:
Atty. MARK BASIL PAPPAS, Esq. of the Law Office of Mark Basil Pappas (Tel: 707-745-8206) for the plaintiff;
Atty. KATHLEEN DARMAGNAC, Esq. of Leone & Alberts (Tel: 925-974-8600) for Defendant
San Francisco Unified School District.

The Court notes that Plaintiff's counsel indicates he did not appear at the regularly scheduled Zoom hearing this morning due to technical issues.

The Court discusses the motion with counsel for both sides for the preparation of the judge pro tem's report & recommendation.

The previously selected continued hearing date of August 4, 2020 remains as set.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-12-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

RULING: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. The complaint alleges that movant is the "owner, operator and manager" of the property where the accident occurred. (Complaint, 5.) The court takes that allegation as true. (See CrossTalk Productions, Inc. v. Jacobson (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th 631, 635 ["A demurrer is treated as admitting all material facts properly pleaded"].) While the complaint also pleads similar allegations against the San Francisco Unified School District at 4, a plaintiff can plead alternative theories of liability. (See Adams v. Paul (1995) 11 Cal.4th 583, 593 [when a pleader is in doubt about what actually occurred or what can be established by the evidence, the modern practice allows that party to plead in the alternative and make inconsistent allegations].) The court grants defendant's request for judicial notice of the SF Charter, which shows that SFUSD and CCSF are distinct entities. (See SF Charter, Art. VIII, 8.100.) Judicially noticeable facts, however, do not show who managed, controlled, or owned the property where the accident occurred. The City raises a factual issue that cannot be resolved on demurrer. (Cf. Soto v. Union Pacific Railroad Co. (2020) 2020 WL 728373 (Jan. 22, 2020) at *5-*6 [trial court properly granted summary judgment because undisputed facts showed railroad did not own, possess or control the crossing].)

Counsel for plaintiff is to prepare a proposed order which repeats the ruling verbatim and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
